perturbation.original_text: “Purpose”: means the exchange of Confidential Information, trade secrets, know -how and samples in order to evaluate and pursue the objective of one or more potential business arrangements with respect to Kenway’s potential to supply the Company with information, products and/or designs that are proprietary to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: “Purpose”: means the exchange of Confidential Information, trade secrets, know -how and samples in order to evaluate and pursue the objective of one or more potential business arrangements with respect to Kenway’s potential to supply the Company with information, products and/or designs that are proprietary to the Company. **However, any actual business arrangement stemming from this evaluation will require a separate legally binding agreement.**
perturbation.explanation: The modification adds a clause stating that any actual business arrangement needs a separate agreement. This clashes with the original definition of 'Purpose,' which suggests the NDA covers the evaluation phase leading to potential business arrangements. The addition creates uncertainty about whether actions taken during the 'evaluation' phase, seemingly covered by the NDA, are actually protected until a new agreement is in place. In this case, a breach done during this 'evaluation' phase, could potentially not be covered by the NDA. The clarification was inserted at the end of the definition in Clause 1.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, shall: (a) not use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this Agreement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictly n ecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of the Recipient; (e) apply the same security meas ures and degree of care to the Confidential Information as the Recipient applies to its own confidential information, which the Recipient warrants as providing adequate protection from unauthori zed disclosure, copying or use; and (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the other in tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Purpose' included the evaluation phase leading to potential business arrangements, implying that the NDA covers this entire process. The changed text adds a clause stating that any actual business arrangement requires a separate agreement. This creates a contradiction because it introduces uncertainty about whether the actions taken during the 'evaluation' phase are protected by the NDA until a new agreement is in place. The Contradicted Text states that the Recipient shall not use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose. The change undermines this, as it is no longer clear whether using Confidential Information during the evaluation phase is fully covered by the original NDA because a separate agreement is required for business arrangements.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, shall: (a) not use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this Agreement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictly n ecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of the Recipient; (e) apply the same security meas ures and degree of care to the Confidential Information as the Recipient applies to its own confidential information, which the Recipient warrants as providing adequate protection from unauthori zed disclosure, copying or use; and (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the other in tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, shall: (a) not use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this Agreement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictly n ecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of the Recipient; (e) apply reasonable security meas ures and a reasonable degree of care to the Confidential Information; (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the other in tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information. **However, in situations where maintaining complete confidentiality proves commercially unviable, the Recipient is only expected to exert best efforts to protect confidential information.**
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a 'best efforts' clause. The original language in 2.1(e) contains the word 'warrants' and means providing adequate protection from unauthorized disclosure, copying or use. Adding the new additional line makes the commitment to confidentiality ambiguous. A dispute could arise over what constitutes 'best efforts' versus 'adequate protection'. The added text after (f) is introducing the uncertainty. This is contradicting the original warrant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) apply reasonable security meas ures and a reasonable degree of care to the Confidential Information
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a 'best efforts' clause to the confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the original text's implication of a higher standard of care, in which the Recipient 'warrants' adequate protection. This introduces ambiguity about the level of protection required, creating an in-text contradiction in section 2.1, and specifically contradicts the original intent of section (e). The Change to the original text creates a new contradiction in section (e), with the addition of 'best efforts', creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient shall: (a) destroy or return to the Disclos ing Party all documents and materials (and any copies) containing, reflecting, incorporating, or based on the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information; (b) save in relation to its backup email systems, erase all the Disclosing Party's Confidential Informati on from its computer systems or which is stored in electronic form ; and (c) certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that it has complied with the requirements of this Clause, provided that a Recipient may retain documents and materials containing, reflectin g, incorporating, or based on the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the extent required by law or any applicable governmental or regulatory authority and to the extent reasonable to permit the Recipient to keep evidence that it has performed i ts obligations under this Agreement. The provisions of this Agreement shall continue to apply to any documents and materials retained by the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient shall: (a) destroy or return to the Disclos ing Party all documents and materials (and any copies) containing, reflecting, incorporating, or based on the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information; (b) save in relation to its backup email systems, erase all the Disclosing Party's Confidential Informati on from its computer systems or which is stored in electronic form ; and (c) certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that it has complied with the requirements of this Clause, provided that a Recipient may retain documents and materials containing, reflectin g, incorporating, or based on the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the extent required by law or any applicable governmental or regulatory authority and to the extent reasonable to permit the Recipient to keep evidence that it has performed i ts obligations under this Agreement. The provisions of this Agreement shall continue to apply to any documents and materials retained by the Recipient. **However, should the Recipient deem the information essential for ongoing business operations, they may retain a copy indefinitely.**
perturbation.explanation: The additional line, 'However, should the Recipient deem the information essential for ongoing business operations, they may retain a copy indefinitely,' creates a contradiction. It allows the Recipient to retain information indefinitely based on their own subjective assessment, overriding the original requirement to destroy or return it at the Disclosing Party's request, the original clause states, that should be destroy or return. Now we have the added exception.This could introduce a dispute over what constitutes 'essential for ongoing business operations'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) destroy or return to the Disclos ing Party all documents and materials (and any copies) containing, reflecting, incorporating, or based on the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new condition allowing the Recipient to retain confidential information indefinitely if they deem it essential for business operations. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Recipient to 'destroy or return' all confidential information at the Disclosing Party's request. The original clause dictates destroy or return of the confidential information, while the changed text now allows the recipient to retain the same information if they deem it essential for ongoing operations. This creates a direct conflict, because the recipient now has the discretion to contradict the will of the disclosing party. The change introduces an ambiguity because 'essential for ongoing business operations' can be subjective and introduce a dispute.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or discussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of 2 years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or discussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of 2 years from the date of this Agreement. **However, any data that falls under the exception described in clause 1.1(g) will not be subject to this timeline.**
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence creates a contradiction. While clause 6.1 dictates a clear 2-year obligation, the new sentence suggests certain data (as defined in modified 1.1(g), which pertains to data shared in public forums) is exempt from this timeline. This introduces uncertainty about which information is actually protected for the full 2 years. Previously, all information would be protected for 2 years. The added condition is contradicting that timeline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or discussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of 2 years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the 2-year obligation, stating that data falling under clause 1.1(g) is not subject to this timeline. This directly contradicts the original clause 6.1 and the contradicted text (which is the same as original clause 6.1), which states that 'The obligations of each Party...continue for a period of 2 years'. The addition creates a conflict because it makes the duration of the obligations uncertain for certain types of data, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Governing Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Delaware, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The Parties irrevocably agree that the Courts of Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Governing Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Delaware, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The Parties irrevocably agree that the Courts of Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims). **However, for disputes concerning intellectual property, the jurisdiction will be where the intellectual property was originally created.**
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence creates a contradiction regarding jurisdiction. While the primary clause specifies Delaware courts for all disputes, the added sentence shifts jurisdiction to the location of intellectual property creation for those specific disputes. This inconsistency can cause legal ambiguity, and delay if there is uncertainty on where to even file a lawsuit. Is the intellectual property dispute tied to the Delaware jurisdiction, or the new addition to where the IP was created. This inconsistency could further delay legal proceedings or cause uncertainty on where to file a dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Governing Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Delaware, of the United States of America, without regard to conflict of law rules thereof. The Parties irrevocably agree that the Courts of Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim that arises out of or in connection with this Agreement or its subject matter or formation (including non -contractual disputes or claims).
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a provision that for intellectual property disputes, jurisdiction will be where the IP was originally created. The contradicted text states that Delaware Courts have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim. This creates a direct contradiction, as intellectual property disputes are a type of dispute that would have fallen under the exclusive jurisdiction of Delaware courts, but now have an alternative jurisdiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as it creates an inconsistency regarding jurisdiction, making enforcement unclear.
